name: Beam Global
business: Beam Global develops, designs, engineers, manufactures, and sells rapidly deployed, autonomous infrastructure products for electric vehicle charging, energy security, battery storage, and smart cities, powered primarily by renewable energy. The company sells its products to U.S. federal, state, and local governments, militaries, corporations, universities, and international customers. Beam also manufactures streetlighting, cell towers, and power electronics through its Serbian subsidiaries.
sellsTo: U.S. Federal Government (including Army, Veterans Affairs, Homeland Security, Marine Corps)
State and local governments (e.g., California, City of Dallas)
Corporations and commercial entities (e.g., Upscale Developers, Advanced Energy Mahine)

exposures.sentence: We develop, design, engineer, manufacture, and sell high-quality, rapidly-deployed and autonomous infrastructure products for electric vehicle ( EV ) and autonomous vehicle ( AV ) charging, energy security and disaster preparedness and highly energy-dense battery solutions
We provide energy storage technologies that make commodity battery cells safer, longer lasting and more energy efficient.
Our EV and AV charging infrastructure products are powered by locally generated and stored renewable energy

BeamPatrol - Rapidly deployed and easily transported, the BeamPatrol station allows law enforcement and safety personnel to charge and quickly access electric motorcycles without the need for any additional infrastructure or fuel.
On August 30, 2024, Beam acquired Telcom d.o.o. Beograd ( Telcom ), a business located in Serbia and engaged in the manufacturing of power electronics and telecommunications equipment.

At the same time, global data center electricity consumption is projected to grow by approximately 15 percent per year from 2024 through 2030, more than four times faster than total electricity demand growth from other sectors.

Our products enable utilities and others to deliver reliable and low-cost electricity to EV and AV chargers and, in the case of a grid failure, to first responders and others, through our integrated emergency power panels.
We believe that combining machine learning with AI in the type of sensor rich environments which we can deploy may create significant revenue opportunities in the future.

catalysts.event: GSA Multiple Award Schedule contract extended until October 31, 2030
catalysts.sentence: In Q2 2025, the contract was extended until October 31, 2030.

catalysts.event: First sponsorship agreement with Globos Osiguranje for Drive on Sunshine network at Belgrade Airport
catalysts.sentence: In November 2024, we announced our first sponsorship agreement with Globos Osiguranje, one of Serbia's premier insurance companies.

catalysts.event: Awarded cooperative purchasing contract by Sourcewell
catalysts.sentence: On November 12, 2025, we announced being awarded a cooperative purchasing contract by Sourcewell, expanding our access to U.S. military, state and local government agencies and higher education institutions across North America.

catalysts.event: First order for BeamSpot product received within two months of launch
catalysts.sentence: We received our first order for that product within two months of it being launched.

catalysts.event: Introduction of BeamWell self-sufficient water treatment system
catalysts.sentence: During the second half of 2024 we significantly expanded our product portfolio with the addition of rapidly deployed and highly scalable charging infrastructure products for electric bicycles, electric scooters, and electric motorcycles. We also introduced de-salination as a capability to enhance the lifesaving aspects of our disaster preparedness products.

tamClaims.claim: Global lithium-ion battery market size projected to grow from $195 billion in 2025 to $426 billion by 2033 at a CAGR of 10%
tamClaims.sentence: According to MarketsandMarkets Analysis, the global lithium-ion battery market size is projected to grow from $195 billion in 2025 to $426 billion by 2033; and it is expected to grow at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 10% from 2025 to 2033.

tamClaims.claim: EV-charging infrastructure market expected to reach $238.8 billion by 2033 at a CAGR of 25% from 2026-2033
tamClaims.sentence: According to Grand View Research, research indicates that the EV-charging infrastructure market is expected to reach $238.8 Billion by 2033 which is a 25% compound annual growth rate (CAGR) between 2026 2033.

tamClaims.claim: California Energy Commission approved $1.4 billion for 17,000 new light-duty chargers
tamClaims.sentence: In December 2024, the California Energy Commission approved a plan for nearly $1.4 billion in funding for 17,000 new light-duty chargers statewide.
